fresh_facts.claim: The US military initiated a naval blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz on April 13, 2026, deploying at least 13 warships to intercept vessels and enforce economic pressure.
US Central Command (CENTCOM) is conducting mine-clearing operations using guided-missile destroyers USS Frank E. Peterson and USS Michael Murphy, with upcoming underwater drones.
Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) warned of a harsh response including mine-laying in the Strait of Hormuz to challenge US forces.
Iranian state media released footage from April 12 showing IRGC claiming missile and drone locks on a US Navy destroyer that forced a retreat, though US CENTCOM denies any standoff occurred.
The IRGC has asserted control over the Strait of Hormuz since February 28, 2026, sporadically attacking ships and laying mines.
US-Iran ceasefire talks in Islamabad ended on April 13, 2026, preceding the US blockade announcement.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei appointed Major General Amir Hatami as overall Artesh (regular military) commander, replacing Major General Abdol Rahim Mousavi.
Hard-line factions in Iran consolidated power and sidelined reformists via Guardian Council vetting since 2020.
Iran maintains an internet blackout lasting 41 days as of early 2026 amid heavy fighting.
The Iranian regime executed 14 people, including seven linked to January 2026 anti-government protests, with arrests and summons ongoing.
Stringent international sanctions persist against Iran due to its anti-Western stance, exacerbating economic isolation.
Severe energy shortages occurred in Iran during winter 2024-2025, worsening inflation and poverty.
Israeli Defense Forces expanded strikes on June 15, 2025, to regime institutions including the Justice and Foreign Affairs Ministries in Tehran and an Artesh airbase near Mashhad.
Israeli strikes on June 14-15, 2025, targeted Iranian energy infrastructure that could deepen energy shortages.
Legal experts affirm the US blockade is lawful under naval warfare rules, allowing inspections of neutral ships and potential boarding or seizure for non-compliance.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces compounding pressures from a sustained US naval blockade (initiated April 13), Israeli airstrikes decimating regime security leadership (June 14-15), and intensified internal repression with over 700 arrests and mass executions by early July, while diplomatic avenues remain closed and succession uncertainty continues amid military command restructuring.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli airstrikes on June 15, 2026, killed IRGC Intelligence Organization Chief Mohammad Kazemi and other senior security officials, targeting key regime institutions (Justice, Foreign Affairs, MOIS, LEC) while Supreme Leader Khamenei replaced the Artesh commander, signaling severe institutional disruption and succession pressures.
